name: Embalse de Villalba de los Barros and Castillo
name_original: Embalse de Villalba de los Barros y Castillo de Feria — circular desde Fuente del Maestre
description: Cycle a difficult 23.2-mile route from Fuente del Maestre, exploring the Embalse de Villalba de los Barros and the historic Castillo de Feri
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Fuente Del Maestre traverse a diverse landscape characterized by extensive olive groves and vineyards. The region features undulating valleys shaped by the Guadajira River and its tributaries, providing varied terrain. It is flanked by the Sierras of San Jorge, Cabrera, and Águila, contributing to the scenic backdrop. Agricultural landscapes with sand tracks are common, offering a mix of surfaces for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Fuente Del Maestre The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Villalba de los Barros and Castillo, a 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes an embalse and a castle, offering a challenging ride through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Los Santos de Maimona – Zafra loop from Fuente del Maestre, a difficult 31.0 miles (49.8 km) path. This route provides expansive views and a long loop through the surrounding area, typically requiring 4 hours 25 minutes. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Los Santos de Maimona – Zafra loop from Cercado del Ángel, a 24.3 miles (39.1 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and offering views, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Touring cycling around Fuente Del Maestre is defined by olive groves, vineyards, and undulating valleys, with routes winding through agricultural areas and near the Sierras. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels, catering to various cycling preferences. The routes in Fuente Del Maestre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.95 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Fuente Del Maestre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Fuente del Maestre?
A: There are over 110 touring cycling routes around Fuente del Maestre listed on komoot, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Fuente del Maestre?
A: Yes, Fuente del Maestre offers several easy touring cycling routes. There are 12 routes specifically categorized as easy, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. One example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafranca de los Barros loop from Villafranca de los Barros&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 14 km.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Fuente del Maestre?
A: The terrain around Fuente del Maestre is diverse, characterized by extensive olive groves and vineyards. You'll encounter undulating valleys shaped by the Guadajira River and its tributaries, with routes winding through agricultural areas. The region is flanked by the Sierras of San Jorge, Cabrera, and Águila, providing scenic backdrops. Some routes may include sand tracks, so caution is advised, especially after rain when conditions can become muddy.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022037625/embalse-de-villalba-de-los-barros-and-castillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Villalba de los Barros and Castillo&lt;/a&gt; route is a circular tour. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Los Santos de Maimona – Zafra loop from Fuente del Maestre&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while cycling?
A: While cycling, you can explore several historical and natural attractions. The town itself is a 'Bien de Interés Cultural' with a rich heritage. You might pass by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parador Nacional Duques de Feria&lt;/a&gt;, a historic castle, or the unique, man-made monument &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4270417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Capricho de Cotrina&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4082806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyards of Ribera del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; also offer picturesque agricultural landscapes.

Q: Is Fuente del Maestre a good destination for family cycling trips?
A: Fuente del Maestre can be suitable for family cycling, especially on its easier routes. With 12 easy routes available, families can find pleasant rides through the rural landscapes. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Fuente del Maestre?
A: Fuente del Maestre experiences a Mediterranean subtropical climate with mild winters and hot, dry summers. The best times for touring cycling are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are more moderate and comfortable for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during this season.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Fuente del Maestre?
A: The touring cycling routes in Fuente del Maestre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.95 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often appreciate the varied terrain, the scenic olive groves and vineyards, and the challenging aspects of some of the longer routes.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes from Fuente del Maestre?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes for touring cyclists seeking a challenge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Los Santos de Maimona – Zafra loop from Fuente del Maestre&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 50 km and typically takes over 4 hours to complete, offering an extensive ride through the region.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Fuente del Maestre?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, Fuente del Maestre is a town with various public parking options. It's advisable to look for parking within the town center or near the starting points of the routes, especially if they begin from urban areas. For routes starting in more rural locations, roadside parking might be available, but always ensure it's safe and permissible.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the cycling paths?
A: As the routes traverse rural landscapes, olive groves, and vineyards, you might encounter common agricultural and Mediterranean wildlife. This could include various bird species, small mammals, and insects. While specific wildlife sightings are not guaranteed, the natural setting provides opportunities to observe local fauna.

Q: Are there routes that pass through or near historical towns like Zafra?
A: Yes, some routes offer the chance to visit nearby historical towns. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Los Santos de Maimona – Zafra loop from Cercado del Ángel&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a route that includes views and passes through the area around Zafra, a town known for its historical significance and attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;town of Zafra&lt;/a&gt; itself and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3804712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Francisco Tower of Zafra&lt;/a&gt;.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is rated as difficult for touring bicycles. It covers approximately 37.3 km with about 550 meters of elevation gain, requiring very good fitness and potentially advanced riding skills. Be prepared for significant climbs and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of surfaces. While some sections may be paved, you should expect unpaved tracks, compacted dirt, grass, and potentially leaf litter, making it more suitable for a touring bicycle capable of handling varied terrain. It's not purely a road cycling route.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable sights, including the serene views of the Villalba de los Barros Dam (Embalse de Villalba de los Barros). A major highlight is the medieval Castillo de Feria, a 15th-century castle with imposing towers. The route also passes through the town of Fuente del Maestre and offers views of pastures and vineyards in the Tierra de Barros region.

faqs.question: Where can I park and what is the starting point for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting and ending in Fuente del Maestre. You can typically find parking within the town, which serves as the most convenient trailhead for this loop.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To avoid extreme heat, the best times to cycle in Extremadura are generally spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the landscape is often at its most vibrant.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on this route, it's primarily a cycling trail with varied terrain and potentially shared paths. If bringing a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, kept on a leash, and capable of keeping pace with a bicycle for the duration of the ride. Be mindful of local livestock in pasture areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route. It traverses public areas and roads in the Extremadura region of Spain.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery, from the tranquil waters of the Embalse de Villalba de los Barros to rolling pastures with sheep farms. You'll also see the historic Castillo de Feria dominating the landscape and pass through areas known for vineyards in the Tierra de Barros region.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant elevation gain, and mixed terrain, this route is not recommended for families with young children or beginner touring cyclists. It requires very good fitness and some experience with varied cycling conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to resupply along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Fuente del Maestre, where you can find amenities like cafes and shops. The town of Villalba de los Barros, near the dam and castle, may also offer options for refreshments or supplies.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, it's advisable to pack plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle (including spare tubes), and appropriate cycling gear. Sun protection is essential, especially during warmer months. A helmet is highly recommended.
